title: Minimal R OME-Zarr Reader
text: can install development version rome like :

title: Minimal R OME-Zarr Reader
text: basic example shows solve common problem:
code: library(rome) x <- ome_read(   system.file("extdata", "ome-v0.4", "10501752.zarr", package = "rome") ) #> Warning in group_attributes$ome: partial match of 'ome' to 'omero' #> Warning in group_attributes$ome: partial match of 'ome' to 'omero' plot(x, all = TRUE)

title: Extract specific levels from a multiscale ome-zarr object — extract_levels
code: x <- ome_read(  system.file("extdata", "ome-v0.4", "10501752.zarr", package = "rome") ) #> Error in read_schema(schema, v8): Schema '' looks like a filename but does not exist extract_levels(x, c(1, 3)) #> Error: object 'x' not found extract_levels(x, 2) #> Error: object 'x' not found

what: Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_read
title: Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_read
Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_read
code: ome_read(path, s3_client = NULL, lazy = FALSE, validate = TRUE)

title: Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_read
text: path Path OME-Zarr file. s3_client Object created paws.storage::s3(). required file S3. Leave NULL file local storage. lazy Logical. TRUE (default), use ZarrArray read data lazily. FALSE, read data memory using Rarr. data can fit memory, setting lazy = FALSE may result better performance. validate Logical.TRUE (default), validate OME-Zarr file.

title: Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_read
code: ome_read(   system.file("extdata", "ome-v0.2", "6001247.zarr", package = "rome"),   validate = FALSE ) #> Error in switch(ome_version, `0.1` = , `0.2` = , `0.3` = , `0.4` = metadata$multiscales,     `0.5` = metadata$ome$multiscales, stop("Unsupported OME version: ",         ome_version)): EXPR must be a length 1 vector  ome_read(  system.file("extdata", "ome-v0.3", "9528933.zarr", package = "rome") ) #> Error in read_schema(schema, v8): Schema '' looks like a filename but does not exist  x <- ome_read(   system.file("extdata", "ome-v0.4", "10501752.zarr", package = "rome") ) #> Error in read_schema(schema, v8): Schema '' looks like a filename but does not exist

title: Validate OME-Zarr file — ome_validate
text: path Path OME-Zarr file. s3_client Object created paws.storage::s3(). required file S3. Leave NULL file local storage.

title: Validate OME-Zarr file — ome_validate
text: function used side-effect return error passed invalid OME-Zarr file

title: Plot an ome_zarr object. — plot.ome_zarr
code: # S3 method for class 'ome_zarr' plot(x, level = 1, ...)

title: Plot an ome_zarr object. — plot.ome_zarr
text: x ome_zarr object. level Integer. scale level plot. Defaults 1 (highest resolution). ... Additional arguments passed plot().

title: Plot an ome_zarr object. — plot.ome_zarr
code: x <- ome_read(   system.file("extdata", "ome-v0.4", "10501752.zarr", package = "rome") ) #> Error in read_schema(schema, v8): Schema '' looks like a filename but does not exist plot(x) #> Error: object 'x' not found

title: Subset an ome-zarr object — [.ome_zarr
text: Subset operation applied levels multiscale ome-zarr object. result ome-zarr object number levels, level subsetted according provided indices.

title: Subset an ome-zarr object — [.ome_zarr
text: x ome-zarr object. ... Indices subset ome-zarr object.

title: Subset an ome-zarr object — [.ome_zarr
text: first image subsetted using provided indices, resulting dimensions used subset remaining levels, conserving scaling factor across levels

title: Subset an ome-zarr object — [.ome_zarr
code: x <- ome_read(   system.file("extdata", "ome-v0.4", "10501752.zarr", package = "rome") ) #> Error in read_schema(schema, v8): Schema '' looks like a filename but does not exist y <- x[3, 1:5, 1:5] #> Error: object 'x' not found extract_levels(y, 2) #> Error: object 'y' not found plot(y, level = 2) #> Error: object 'y' not found
